Title: Genome-Wide Identification of JRL Genes in Moso Bamboo and Their Expression Profiles in Response to Multiple Hormones and Abiotic Stresses

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: PeJRL04 was used as a representative single jacalin-domain JRL for comparison with the chimeric dirigent–jacalin protein PeJRL13 in structural modeling, dimerization, and localization analyses.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. PeJRL04 was identified as a jacalin-related lectin gene in Phyllostachys edulis carrying a single jacalin domain and located on scaffold S3.
2. qRT-PCR showed that PeJRL04 expression is downregulated by abscisic acid and shows a delayed response to salicylic acid, while being upregulated under drought stress.
3. 3D structural prediction indicates that PeJRL04 forms a typical jacalin β-prism monomer.
4. Yeast two-hybrid assays demonstrated that PeJRL04 does not self-interact, in contrast to PeJRL13.
5. Subcellular localization assays revealed that PeJRL04 accumulates mainly in the nucleus and cell membrane, with a small amount in the nucleoplasm.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PeJRL04 encodes a jacalin-related lectin localized mainly to the nucleus and cell membrane that is transcriptionally responsive to drought and hormone treatments but does not form a self-interacting homodimer in yeast, indicating a monomeric JRL involved in stress and hormone response signaling.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: No PeJRL04–PeJRL04 interaction detected in yeast two-hybrid assays, indicating that PeJRL04 does not form homodimers under the tested conditions and likely acts as a monomeric jacalin lectin.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Jacalin-related lectin (JRL) with a single jacalin domain (type I JRL)
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Predominantly localized to the nucleus and cell membrane, with a small amount in the nucleoplasm, as determined by 35S:PeJRL04-GFP fusion transiently expressed in tobacco epidermal cells and observed by confocal microscopy.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: qRT-PCR showed that PeJRL04 expression responds to hormone and abiotic treatments in moso bamboo leaves: it displays a delayed response to salicylic acid (SA) treatment, with changes evident after 24 h; its transcript level is downregulated at almost all time points after abscisic acid (ABA) treatment; under drought stress simulated by 30% PEG6000, PeJRL04 expression is upregulated compared with 0 h controls.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salicylic acid (100 μM foliar spray), abscisic acid (100 μM foliar spray), drought stress induced by 30% PEG6000 irrigation
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response and hormone-responsive jacalin-related lectin gene family in moso bamboo
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress (drought stress) and hormone response (salicylic acid response, abscisic acid response)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Moso bamboo (Phyllostachys edulis) seedlings grown from seeds collected in Guilin, Guangxi, China; 1‑month‑old seedlings with roots, stems, young leaves, and mature leaves sampled; Nicotiana tabacum (tobacco) epidermal cells for transient 35S:PeJRL04-GFP expression; Agrobacterium tumefaciens GV3101; yeast strain AH109 for yeast two-hybrid assays
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide identification using HMMER against the Phyllostachys edulis genome; gene cloning and seamless insertion of PeJRL04 CDS into pCAMBIA1300-35S-GFP and Y2H vectors; hormone and abiotic stress treatments of seedlings; RNA extraction, cDNA synthesis, and qRT-PCR (2^-ΔΔCt) to analyze expression under SA, ABA, MeJA, drought (PEG6000), salt (NaCl), and cold; AlphaFold2-based 3D structure prediction; yeast two-hybrid assays in AH109; confocal microscopy of GFP fusion proteins for subcellular localization
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression analysis under hormone (SA, ABA) and drought stress treatments; yeast two-hybrid assay testing PeJRL04–PeJRL04 self-interaction (no homodimerization detected); subcellular localization of 35S:PeJRL04-GFP fusion in tobacco epidermal cells; 3D structural prediction of jacalin domain
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a member of the PeJRL family by HMMER search using the jacalin domain HMM (Pfam01419) against the Phyllostachys edulis proteome, followed by domain confirmation in SMART/Pfam/InterPro; full-length CDS cloned from moso bamboo for fusion expression and yeast two-hybrid assays
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: As a drought- and hormone-responsive nuclear and membrane-localized jacalin-related lectin with defined molecular properties, PeJRL04 represents a potential candidate marker or engineering target for modulating drought stress and hormone signaling responses in moso bamboo, providing a reference for selecting stress-related lectin genes for future breeding or biotechnological improvement.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: PeJRL13 is a dirigent–jacalin (DJRL) chimeric protein predicted to form a dimer with two glycan-binding sites per monomer. It is homologous to wheat TaJRL21, with up to 70% similarity, and to previously described moso bamboo dirigent–jacalin genes PeD-J02 and PeD-J03, which are also MeJA-responsive.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. PeJRL13 was identified as a dirigent–jacalin chimeric JRL gene in Phyllostachys edulis, carrying both jacalin and dirigent domains and located on scaffold S12.
2. Structural modeling predicted that PeJRL13 forms a dimer composed of jacalin and dirigent monomers, each containing two sugar-binding sites.
3. qRT-PCR analysis showed that PeJRL13 expression is highest in stems and is induced by drought and salt stress, while being repressed by salicylic acid and methyl jasmonate treatments.
4. Yeast two-hybrid assays demonstrated that PeJRL13 can self-interact to form a homodimer in yeast strain AH109.
5. Subcellular localization assays revealed that PeJRL13 is distributed in the cell membrane, nucleus, and cytoplasm when expressed as a GFP fusion in tobacco epidermal cells.
6. Combined evidence suggests that PeJRL13 participates in abiotic stress responses in moso bamboo in the form of a homodimeric dirigent–jacalin lectin.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PeJRL13 encodes a dirigent–jacalin chimeric lectin that localizes to the cell membrane, nucleus, and cytoplasm and forms a self-interacting homodimer; its transcript is induced by drought and salt stress and repressed by salicylic acid and methyl jasmonate, supporting a role in abiotic stress and hormone response pathways in moso bamboo.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: PeJRL13 forms a self-interacting homodimer via protein–protein interaction demonstrated by yeast two-hybrid assays, consistent with its predicted dirigent–jacalin dimeric structure and suggesting that homodimerization is important for its role in abiotic stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Dirigent–jacalin chimeric jacalin-related lectin (DJRL), containing both jacalin and dirigent domains and forming a predicted dimer with two glycan-binding sites per monomer
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Localized to the cell membrane, nucleus, and cytoplasm (nucleoplasm), as determined by transient expression of 35S:PeJRL13-GFP in tobacco epidermal cells followed by confocal microscopy.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PeJRL13 (self) – PeJRL13–PeJRL13 homodimer formation detected in yeast two-hybrid assays in strain AH109.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: qRT-PCR showed that PeJRL13 expression is highest in stems among the tested tissues (roots, stems, young leaves, mature leaves). Under hormone treatments, PeJRL13 is downregulated or not expressed at early time points after salicylic acid treatment and its expression is inhibited by methyl jasmonate. Under abiotic stress, PeJRL13 is upregulated by drought stress (30% PEG6000) and is reported to be induced by salt stress, consistent with its classification as a stress-responsive dirigent–jacalin lectin.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salicylic acid (100 μM foliar spray), methyl jasmonate (100 μM foliar spray), abscisic acid (100 μM, tested at the family level), drought stress induced by 30% PEG6000 irrigation, and salt stress induced by 200 mM NaCl
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response, hormone-responsive jacalin-related lectin gene family, and functional characterization of dirigent–jacalin chimeric lectins in moso bamboo
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress (drought stress, salt stress) and hormone response (salicylic acid response, methyl jasmonate response)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Moso bamboo (Phyllostachys edulis) seedlings grown from seeds collected in Guilin, Guangxi, China; 1‑month‑old seedlings with roots, stems, young leaves, and mature leaves sampled; Nicotiana tabacum epidermal cells for transient 35S:PeJRL13-GFP expression; Agrobacterium tumefaciens GV3101; yeast strain AH109 for yeast two-hybrid assays
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide identification of PeJRL genes using HMMER against the P. edulis genome and domain confirmation; cloning of full-length PeJRL13 CDS into pCAMBIA1300-35S-GFP and Y2H vectors; hormone (SA, ABA, MeJA) and abiotic (drought with 30% PEG6000, 200 mM NaCl, 4°C cold) treatments of seedlings; RNA extraction, cDNA synthesis, and qRT-PCR (2^-ΔΔCt) to analyze expression; AlphaFold2-based 3D structural modeling; yeast two-hybrid assays to test PeJRL13–PeJRL13 interaction; confocal microscopy of 35S:PeJRL13-GFP in tobacco epidermal cells for subcellular localization.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling under hormone (SA, ABA, MeJA) and abiotic (drought, salt) treatments; yeast two-hybrid assays demonstrating PeJRL13–PeJRL13 self-interaction; subcellular localization of 35S:PeJRL13-GFP fusion in tobacco epidermal cells; AlphaFold2-based 3D structural modeling of the dirigent–jacalin dimer.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a JRL family member by HMMER search using the jacalin domain HMM (Pfam01419) on the Phyllostachys edulis proteome, followed by verification of jacalin and dirigent domains; cloned full-length CDS from moso bamboo for Y2H and GFP-fusion localization experiments.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because PeJRL13 is strongly induced by drought and salt stress, repressed by defense-related hormones, localized to key cellular compartments, and forms a homodimer, it represents a promising stress-related candidate gene for future molecular breeding or genetic engineering aimed at improving drought and salt tolerance in moso bamboo or for developing markers associated with stress-resistance pathways.
